ogTitle: McBean Pound vs McCracken: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in McBean Pound and McCracken.
heading: McBean Pound vs McCracken — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between McBean Pound, South Australia and McCracken, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: McBean Pound has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to McCracken (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is McBean Pound or McCracken safer from flooding?
faq.answer: McBean Pound has a flood risk score of 25/100, while McCracken scores 54/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: McBean Pound scores 18/100 for bushfire risk. McCracken scores 20/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in McBean Pound or McCracken?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. McBean Pound has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
